purpose: Known risks, limitations, provisional states, and upstream blockers for the SDK/CLI/MCP/OpenAPI platform. Every entry needs evidence, mitigation, and a closure gate.

contains: Does not run Git, npm, Docker, Fern, tests, builds, or Clockify API calls
has passing measurements but embedded calibrationPolicy.status

risks.summary: Historical Fern CLI bare-array pagination limitation is no longer an active blocker because the required SDK generator is local; wrapper pagination helpers remain the supported public surface.
risks.impact: No active generator blocker. Generated list methods still need to preserve page/page-size request shapes consumed by iterAll, iterPages, and paginate.
risks.mitigation: Keep pagination wrappers tested and keep local generator comparison/drift checks active.
risks.closureGate: No closure planned; keep as historical evidence unless the pagination helper surface is intentionally redesigned.

risks.summary: The historical Fern addon-token workaround is retired; local generated auth types now model exactly one token mode.
risks.impact: A regression could reintroduce workaround casts or allow callers to send both Clockify auth headers.
risks.mitigation: Keep generated HeaderAuthProvider auth options mutually exclusive and keep createClockifyClient auth tests active.
risks.closureGate: No closure planned; keep discrepancy evidence and SDK auth tests as regression coverage.

risks.summary: Live tests intentionally mutate Clockify state and must run only against a sacrificial sandbox workspace.
risks.impact: Live proof is high value but unsafe against customer workspaces.
risks.mitigation: Require explicit env vars and document sandbox-only usage before running live gates.
risks.closureGate: Keep live proof receipts showing sandbox cleanup; never generalize live gates to customer workspaces.

evidence.contains: Never run live gates against a customer workspace
make perfect-live        Explicit sandbox/live cleanup proof. Requires live env vars.

risks.summary: Packages are published to npm under the unofficial @apet97 scope; publication stays tag-gated CI, so npm publication is not the default path for routine changes and the publish manifest fields + prepublishOnly gates remain load-bearing.
risks.impact: Publishing is a deliberate tag push (wrapper-v*/cli-v*/mcp-v*); the prepublishOnly gates and tag-vs-version guards are the safety rails. Re-publishing on every change is not the workflow.
risks.mitigation: Keep release/support policy, package contract, and CI contract aligned with the tag-gated publish stance.
risks.closureGate: Only publish after explicit maintainer approval plus perfect-full, pack-smoke, and release/support proof.

evidence.contains: npm publication is not the default path

risks.summary: Tag-triggered npm release workflows publish all three packages on prefixed tags (wrapper-v*/cli-v*/mcp-v*); the maintainer reviewed and enabled them (release-please files the wrapper version-bump PR; release.yml/ci-cli-release.yml/ci-mcp-release.yml each publish on a tag whose version matches its package.json).
risks.impact: Release automation still requires explicit maintainer approval per publish; local packable readiness is not the same as permission to publish. The tag-vs-version guard remains load-bearing.
risks.mitigation: Keep the no-default-publish policy, package contracts, CI contract, and release-decision planner visible; do not run or modify release automation casually. release-please can file PRs after enabling the repo-level `can_approve_pull_request_reviews` setting.
risks.closureGate: Maintainer reviewed release.yml + release-please.yml on 2026-05-28; on 2026-06-28 the maintainer enabled tag-triggered npm publish for all three packages under the unofficial @apet97 scope (CLI/MCP scaffolds flipped from inert to cli-v*/mcp-v* publishers; SDK moved from bare v* to wrapper-v* tags). The tag-vs-version guard remains load-bearing.

risks.summary: Generated code is a replaceable lower layer, not the product surface users should depend on directly.
risks.impact: Direct imports from generated internals make generator replacement and OpenAPI regeneration brittle.
risks.mitigation: Keep generated-edit, generator-independence, dependency-boundary, and SDK public API gates active.
risks.closureGate: No closure planned; this is a permanent architecture constraint.

risks.summary: Live read-only proof established that the expense route ignores start/end while honoring pagination; SDK, CLI, and MCP now share one bounded client-side filter contract.
risks.impact: Date-filtered reads can require multiple API pages and may stop at the configured bound, so consumers must inspect the warning and continuation metadata.
risks.mitigation: Keep listExpensesFiltered shared across CLI/MCP, preserve the typed double-nested envelope, and retain the bounded scan plus client-side warning.
risks.closureGate: Closed 2026-07-19 by the sanitized Task 2 live receipt, focused wrapper/CLI/MCP tests, consumer-cast budget, operation-parity drift, and contract gates.

risks.summary: The generated expense-update request shape still exposes a multipart file field that the supported scalar update workflow does not model truthfully.
risks.impact: Callers may need an unsafe request cast or may assume a file-upload update shape that the supported workflow cannot prove.
risks.mitigation: Keep the explicit scalar-versus-multipart mismatch visible until Task 3 corrects the contract and removes the unsupported path.
risks.closureGate: `make risk-register` (including `scripts/check-risk-register.readiness.test.mjs`), `make sdk-codegen-test consumer-cast-budget`, `npm test -w @apet97/clockify-cli-115 -- tests/read-commands-expenses.test.ts`, and `make contract-gates`.

evidence.contains: expense update scalar shape omits the generated multipart file

risks.summary: The parity inventory does not yet prove that every claimed generated operation is reachable through the intended public SDK surface.
risks.impact: A parity row can look covered while the generated method is missing, inaccessible, or only present behind an unsupported path.
risks.mitigation: Treat generated inventory markers as orientation only until Task 5 adds reachability proof and regenerates the parity surface.
risks.closureGate: `make risk-register` (including `scripts/check-risk-register.readiness.test.mjs`), `make sdk-codegen sdk-codegen-drift operation-parity-drift contract-gates`, and `docs/roadmap-1.0-receipts/task-05-generated-reachability.md`.

risks.summary: Consumer request casts remain a temporary escape hatch instead of a zero-cast public request boundary.
risks.impact: A generated schema mismatch can be hidden at the CLI or MCP boundary and reach a live request without type-level protection.
risks.mitigation: Keep the cast budget at the existing ratchet and close this blocker only after Task 7 removes or proves every remaining request cast.
risks.closureGate: `make risk-register` (including `scripts/check-risk-register.readiness.test.mjs`), `make consumer-cast-budget contract-gates`, `npm test -w @apet97/clockify-cli-115`, `npm test -w @apet97/clockify-mcp-115`, and `docs/roadmap-1.0-receipts/task-07-zero-cast.md`.

risks.summary: The three npm packages do not yet share one exact-artifact release proof that demonstrates equivalent consumer-install evidence.
risks.impact: One package can look ready while another lacks the packed-consumer or release-proof evidence needed for a coordinated 1.0 claim.
risks.mitigation: Keep release readiness blocked until Tasks 9 through 13 establish the shared artifact engine, all three adoptions, and the manual governance receipt.
risks.closureGate: `make risk-register` (including `scripts/check-risk-register.readiness.test.mjs`), `make perfect-full pack-smoke release-readiness`, and `docs/roadmap-1.0-receipts/task-13-exact-artifact.md` naming all three tarball digests and consumer outputs.

risks.summary: Required mutation proof has not yet been executed and retained through the GitHub-only workflow for the planned 1.0 scope.
risks.impact: Local configuration or static wiring can be green while the governed mutation targets have no remote execution receipt.
risks.mitigation: Use only the manual GitHub Mutation workflow for the 1.0 proof; do not run local Stryker or a mutation package command for this execution.
risks.closureGate: `make risk-register` (including `scripts/check-risk-register.readiness.test.mjs`), `make mutation-ci`, and `docs/roadmap-1.0-receipts/task-18-remote-mutation.md` with GitHub Actions Mutation run URLs plus retained mutation-report artifact names for every approved target.
